* fix: guard sharpe ratio against single-observation std dev (ddof=1 with n=1)

Require at least 2 return observations before calling np.std(ddof=1).
With only 1 return value, numpy emits a RuntimeWarning (degrees of
freedom <= 0) and produces NaN. Returns None early instead.

commit.message:
perf: avoid HashSet allocation in role requirement handlers (#221)

Replace Intersect().Any() with Any(Contains) in RealmAccessRequirementHandler
and ResourceAccessRequirementHandler. The Enumerable.Intersect() method
internally allocates a HashSet on every call, which is unnecessary overhead
in the hot authorization path (called once per authenticated request per
protected endpoint).

For typical role lists (1-10 items), direct Contains checks are faster
due to lower allocation pressure and better cache locality.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  with a single foreach over the claims collection. The JSON claim is
  detected eagerly and short-circuits the loop; string aliases are
  lazily accumulated in a List<string>? to avoid allocation when there
  are no matching claims at all.

- VerificationPlan: change the internal scopes store from
  Dictionary<string,List<string>> to Dictionary<string,string[]>. This
  lets GetEnumerator() pass the stored array directly to
  ProtectedResourceAttribute instead of calling .ToArray() on every
  iteration, eliminating one heap allocation per resource per
  enumeration. The Add() path handles the rare multi-scope case with a
  simple array copy.

commit.message:
feat(authorization): add Audience override for per-resource UMA ticket exchange (#135) (#246)

Allows a single .NET API to enforce policies from multiple Keycloak
resource server clients (same realm) by specifying an optional Audience
on ProtectedResourceAttribute and DecisionRequirement.

When Audience is set, it is forwarded through ProtectedResourceVerifier
and IAuthorizationServerClient down to AuthorizationServerClient.PrepareRequest,
which uses it as the UMA 'audience' parameter instead of the global
KeycloakAuthorizationServerOptions.Resource value.

When Audience is null, behaviour is fully backward-compatible.

eng: add startup validators for Admin and Protection client options (#239)

Add IValidateOptions<T> implementations for KeycloakAdminClientOptions and
KeycloakProtectionClientOptions, following the existing pattern established
by KeycloakTokenIntrospectionOptionsValidator.

- KeycloakAdminClientOptionsValidator: validates AuthServerUrl is a valid
  absolute URI and Realm is configured
- KeycloakProtectionClientOptionsValidator: same checks for the protection
  client
- Register validators with .ValidateOnStart() so misconfiguration is caught
  at app startup rather than on first HTTP request
- Add unit tests covering success and failure paths

Previously, misconfigured options would throw a NullReferenceException inside
the HttpClient factory when the first request was made (the code used
AuthServerUrl! null-forgiving). Now developers get a clear, actionable error
message at startup.

commit.message:
fix: handle async FSWatcher errors in project customization watcher

On Linux, fs.watch() on a non-existent directory emits an asynchronous
'error' event instead of throwing synchronously. Without an error handler,
this crashes the process. Tests that create AryxAppService with fake
project paths (e.g. C:\workspace\project-one) trigger this on Linux CI
because those paths don't exist.

Attach an 'error' event handler to the FSWatcher that logs a warning and
closes the watcher gracefully. This fixes the Linux CI 'bun test' failure
and also prevents production crashes if a watched directory is deleted
at runtime.

[test] Fix ValueError in test_ecn_config_utility when ecnconfig output has empty lines (#23722)

### Description of PR

Summary:
Fix `ValueError: not enough values to unpack` crash in
`test_ecn_config_utility` when `ecnconfig -l` command output contains
empty lines.

When parsing the output of `ecnconfig -l`, the code splits each line
with `line.split(maxsplit=1)` and unpacks into two variables. If the
output contains empty or whitespace-only lines, the split produces fewer
than 2 values, causing a `ValueError`. This adds an empty-line guard to
skip such lines.

Fixes AB#37432210


```
ecnconfig -l

"stdout_lines": [
    "Profile: WRED_LOSSLESS_Q3",
    "-----------------------  -------",
    "ecn                      ecn_all",
    "green_drop_probability   5",
    "green_max_threshold      262144",
    "green_min_threshold      131072",
    "red_drop_probability     5",
    "red_max_threshold        262144",
    "red_min_threshold        131072",
    "wred_green_enable        true",
    "wred_red_enable          true",
    "wred_yellow_enable       true",
    "yellow_drop_probability  5",
    "yellow_max_threshold     262144",
    "yellow_min_threshold     131072",
    "-----------------------  -------",
    "",
    "Profile: WRED_LOSSLESS_Q4",
    "-----------------------  -------",
    "ecn                      ecn_all",
    "green_drop_probability   5",
    "green_max_threshold      262144",
    "green_min_threshold      131072",
    "red_drop_probability     5",
    "red_max_threshold        262144",
    "red_min_threshold        131072",
    "wred_green_enable        true",
    "wred_red_enable          true",
    "wred_yellow_enable       true",
    "yellow_drop_probability  5",
    "yellow_max_threshold     262144",
    "yellow_min_threshold     131072",
    "-----------------------  -------"
  ],
```

### Type of change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Testbed and Framework(new/improvement)
- [ ] New Test case
    - [ ] Skipped for non-supported platforms
- [ ] Test case improvement

### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [ ] 202511

### Approach
#### What is the motivation for this PR?

`test_ecn_config_utility` in `tests/qos/test_ecn_config.py` crashes
with:
```
ValueError: not enough values to unpack (expected 2, got 1)
```
when `ecnconfig -l` output contains empty or whitespace-only lines. The
parser at line ~278 does:
```python
field, value = line.split(maxsplit=1)
```
which fails on empty lines.

#### How did you do it?

Added `or not line.strip()` to the existing skip condition so
empty/whitespace-only lines are filtered out before the split:

**Before:**
```python
if iter < 2 or '---' in line:
    continue
```

**After:**
```python
if iter < 2 or '---' in line or not line.strip():
    continue
```

#### How did you verify/test it?

- Code inspection confirms the guard handles empty strings,
whitespace-only strings, and newline-only strings
- The change is a single-line addition that does not alter behavior for
non-empty lines

#### Any platform specific information?

N/A

N/A

commit.message:
feat: add client_secret_jwt sample (#174) (#245)

Adds a self-contained sample demonstrating Keycloak's 'Signed JWT with
Client Secret' (client_secret_jwt) authentication method.

Instead of sending the raw client secret over the wire, the app builds a
short-lived HS256-signed JWT assertion via Duende's IClientAssertionService
extension point. The secret never leaves the application — only the signed
JWT is transmitted to Keycloak's token endpoint.

The sample includes:
- ClientSecretJwtAssertionService implementing IClientAssertionService
- Keycloak realm seeding via --import-realm (works out of the box)
- docker-compose.yml with Keycloak 26.5.6
- /realm and /users endpoints calling the Admin API with the obtained token
- README with diagrams and quick start guide
- Docs page added to the examples section

Closes #174

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[KeyBot] feat: add AdditionalAudiences to KeycloakAuthenticationOptions (#235)

* feat: add AdditionalAudiences to KeycloakAuthenticationOptions (#135)

Adds AdditionalAudiences property to KeycloakAuthenticationOptions, allowing
multiple valid JWT audiences to be configured alongside the primary one.
When set, TokenValidationParameters.ValidAudiences is populated with all
audiences (primary + additional), enabling scenarios where tokens may be
issued for multiple clients in the same realm.

No breaking changes — existing single-audience configuration is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: set ValidAudience on TVP directly to prevent loss on TVP replacement

Previously, options.Audience was assigned before options.TokenValidationParameters
was replaced with a new instance. Since JwtBearerOptions.Audience is a pass-through
to TokenValidationParameters.ValidAudience, the assignment was silently lost when
the TVP was replaced.

Fix: set ValidAudience directly on the new TVP instance. When AdditionalAudiences
are provided, use ValidAudiences instead.

Added regression tests to prevent recurrence.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: also set options.Audience after TVP replacement for API consistency

The previous fix set TVP.ValidAudience directly in the initializer but did
not set options.Audience, which is a separate property on JwtBearerOptions.
Integration tests assert on options.Audience directly.

Now both are set: ValidAudience in the TVP initializer (avoids ordering issue)
and options.Audience after TVP creation (maintains API surface).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(sidebar): use counts and minimal-list APIs DEV-1772 (#6918)

### 📣 Summary

Use new API for Sidebar lists of projects (Deployed, Draft, and
Archived). This fixes a problem when user with more than 200 projects
was unable to see all of their projects in the Sidebar (previously it
was limited to 200 latest projects, prioritizing Deployed ones). Now
three groups use infinite scroll loading. We also fixed a problem with
sidebar serving always "My Projects" projects - now when user switches
to organization projects or a custom view, the sidebar reflects this
properly.

### 💭 Notes

Changes here:
- `AssetName` and `getAssetDisplayName` now works with
`AssetMinimalList`
- `BulkDeletePrompt` invalidates all sidebar related queries now
- `SidebarFormsList` has multiple changes:
- It now operates on context (`my-projects`, `my-org-projects`, or
`custom-view-projects`) which is resolved from current path
  - Has a utility function for invalidating all queries in sidebar
- Has a custom `useQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that operates
on different function based on context
  - Reflux actions are now invalidating with utility function
- It now only fetches counts from new APIs (different counts API for
different context) and passes those to `SidebarFormsListCategory`
- `SidebarFormsListCategory` has multiple changes too:
  - It operates now on context and deployment status
- Instead of getting list of assets from parent, it fetches it now using
API
- Has a custom `useInfiniteQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that
operates on different function based on context and is triggered by
`InfiniteScrollTrigger`
- The list of projects now always lead to `ROUTES.FORM`, as minimal list
endpoint doesn't serve permissions

### 👀 Preview steps

1. ℹ️ Have an account with a lot of projects, ideally over 200 (use
kobo-fixture, or drag and drop a lot of small projects [many
small.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26484188/many.small.zip)
or some other idea)
2. [Enable
MMO](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Setting-up-MMOs-for-local-development-1357e515f6548093b631f1686b60e49d)
3. Create new user, add it to MMO, create few projects with the new
user, go back to original user afterwards
4. [Enable a custom
view](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Regional-Projects-User-Documentation-Setting-up-Custom-Project-Views-f45b8a2bd09d45a4ade89f4b4430df1d)
with only part of projects being included (see "South Asia" example in
the link)
5. Change few projects to have the "Country" field matching the custom
view
6. Go to My Projects
7. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/assets/?q=asset_type:survey&limit=200` is no longer being called
8. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/counts/` is being called
9. Open "Deployed" category
10. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/minimal-list/` is being
called and a list of projects appear
11. Scroll down
12. 🟢 Notice that another API call is being made and next 20 projects
are loaded (infinite scroll)
13. Select one project in the table (with checkbox)
14. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
15. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
16. Select two projects in the table (also with checkbox; bulk deleting
uses different code)
17. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
18. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
19. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "<username>'s organization
projects"
20. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different (now it
includes projects from step (3))
21. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
22. Open "Deployed" category
23. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
24. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above
25. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "South Asia" custom view
26. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different
27. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
28. Open "Deployed" category
29. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
30. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above
